result.summary: Atlassian.net exposes a required-auth Atlassian GraphQL API (OAuth 2.0 bearer on `https://api.atlassian.com/graphql` or browser-session/API-token basic auth on tenant gateways), Jira Cloud REST API v3 and Confluence Cloud REST API v2 (Atlassian API token basic auth or Atlassian OAuth 2.0), Bitbucket Cloud REST API (Bitbucket access token bearer or app password basic auth), and a Forge CLI login flow via `forge login` for Forge tooling.

atlassian_oauth2.label: Atlassian OAuth 2.0 client
atlassian_oauth2.setup: Create or use an app in Atlassian’s developer docs, then complete the OAuth 2.0 flow for Atlassian Cloud products. Start from the [OAuth docs](https://developer.atlassian.com/cloud/oauth/) and register the client there if needed. Use this when a surface says OAuth clients should call the tenantless gateway like `https://api.atlassian.com/graphql` or when a product API asks for OAuth 2.0. The exact scopes depend on the product API.

atlassian_api_token.label: Atlassian API token
atlassian_api_token.setup: Create an API token in your Atlassian account at [API tokens](https://id.atlassian.com/manage-profile/security/api-tokens). Use your Atlassian email as the username and the token as the password for basic auth against Atlassian Cloud REST APIs. This is the easiest direct-API credential for personal scripting.

bitbucket_access_token.label: Bitbucket access token
bitbucket_access_token.setup: Create a repository, project, or workspace access token from Bitbucket settings at [Access tokens](https://bitbucket.org/account/settings/access-tokens/). Choose the scope that matches the workspace/repository you need. Bitbucket REST APIs accept these tokens directly as bearer-style credentials for API calls and tooling.

bitbucket_app_password.label: Bitbucket app password
bitbucket_app_password.setup: Create an app password in [Bitbucket settings](https://bitbucket.org/account/settings/app-passwords/) and pair it with your Bitbucket username for HTTP basic auth against Bitbucket Cloud REST APIs. Use this for direct API scripting when you don't want OAuth.

trello_api_key.label: Trello API key
trello_api_key.setup: Get your key from [Trello Power-Up / API key page](https://trello.com/app-key). The page shows your personal API key; use it with Trello REST requests, usually together with a token.

trello_token.label: Trello API token
trello_token.setup: From the same [Trello key page](https://trello.com/app-key), generate an API token tied to your account and a specific app authorization. Use it alongside `key` for Trello REST calls.

forge_cli_login.label: Forge CLI login session
forge_cli_login.setup: Install the Forge CLI from the [Forge CLI docs](https://developer.atlassian.com/platform/forge/cli/) and run `forge login` to authenticate the CLI to your Atlassian account. This is the easiest path for Forge development and deployment flows.
